title: Phishing triage and mailbox containment
prompt: A synthetic user reports an invoice-themed message with a lookalike sender, a blocked attachment, and two recipients who clicked the link.

artifacts.name: Synthetic message header
artifacts.content: from=billing-update@example.net reply_to=payroll-help@example.org subject='Invoice adjustment' spf=softfail dmarc=fail received_ip=192.0.2.44

artifacts.name: Synthetic user report
artifacts.content: maria.santos@example.com reports unexpected invoice email; no payment was made; link opened in browser before warning page appeared.

questions.prompt: What should the triage classification be?

options.label: Phishing
options.text: Classify as a synthetic phishing campaign requiring mailbox and click-scope triage.

options.label: Spam only
options.text: Classify as low-risk spam because the attachment was blocked.

options.label: Confirmed malware
options.text: Classify as confirmed malware execution without endpoint evidence.

questions.explanation: The failed authentication, suspicious attachment, and clicks justify phishing triage even though the attachment was blocked.

citations.title: Synthetic Phishing Triage Lab
citations.excerpt: Synthetic phishing labs use fictional messages, safe domains, and blocked payloads to practice defensive triage.

options.label: Quarantine
options.text: Quarantine matching messages from synthetic mailboxes.

options.label: Identify clickers
options.text: Identify clicked recipients and check synthetic sign-in/session activity.

options.label: Preserve header
options.text: Preserve message headers, gateway verdict, and user report.

options.label: Publish passwords
options.text: Post user passwords to the incident channel.

questions.explanation: The response should remove exposure, scope clicked users, and preserve evidence without unsafe credential handling.

citations.title: Synthetic Incident Triage Playbook
citations.excerpt: Use synthetic alerts to practice severity, scope, containment, evidence preservation, and escalation decisions.

questions.prompt: Write a manager-safe update for this phishing triage.
questions.explanation: A manager-safe update should summarize containment, scope, and evidence without exposing private learner details.

citations.title: Synthetic Manager Readiness Dashboard Brief
citations.excerpt: Manager summaries should aggregate readiness and capacity patterns without exposing private learner weaknesses.

learner_task: Classify the phishing alert, choose safe triage actions, and draft a manager-safe update.
expected_investigation_path: Classify the message as synthetic phishing.
Preserve message headers, gateway verdicts, and user reports.
Quarantine matching messages and identify clicked recipients.
Report scope and containment without real PII or secrets.

rubric.expected_signal: Names phishing and avoids overclaiming malware execution.
Quarantines messages, scopes clicked recipients, and preserves evidence.
Summarizes containment and scope without exposing private data.

safety_note: Synthetic defensive phishing triage only. Do not request real payloads, credentials, or tenant data.

citations.title: Synthetic Phishing Triage Lab
citations.excerpt: Synthetic phishing labs use fictional messages, safe domains, and blocked payloads to practice defensive triage.

citations.title: Synthetic Incident Triage Playbook
citations.excerpt: Use synthetic alerts to practice severity, scope, containment, evidence preservation, and escalation decisions.
